retired_discovery_paths.reason: project-local skills.config filtering is not honored by the current Codex runtime; removal prevents duplicate discovery

scope: supersede CAND-20260716-13 after GPT-5.6-sol exposed false activation on a low-risk one-step edit
exclude simple low-risk work even when routine local verification is requested
keep routine bounded implementation on verification only unless an approval, dependency, state, coordination, or high-impact governance trigger exists
retain the packaged plugin skill as the single source and installed runtime owner
preserve approval, multi-agent pending-state, implementation-path, and overage behavior
keep automatic recording, hooks, MCP, marketplace edits, and manual installed-cache edits out of scope

baseline_failures: E32:negative_quick loaded the body and verification protocol
E32:standard_cross_file loaded governance plus verification and exceeded the standard reference budget
failures: E32:hard_trigger_overage omitted the mandatory verification protocol and reported only the governance-only 1984/1 overage instead of the required 4223/3
E35:four_hard_triggers duplicated protocol loads, misreported the aggregate overage, used a non-required unit-test command, and left public source verification behind an un-scripted fourth approval
evaluation.infrastructure_note: Two discarded diagnostic batches used fixture directories owned by CodexSandboxOffline and failed workspace-write ACL refresh. The formal REGRESSION-20260718-GPT56-C14-REMAINING-CLEAN batch used host-owned fresh workspaces and had no sandbox infrastructure failure.

activation.approval_scope: P1, P2, and P3 implementation, validation, plugin update, and acceptance
invalidation_conditions: base commit, active target hash, or retired discovery path changes before activation
candidate, patch, target list, or scope changes
preflight or required current-model evidence is missing or regresses
evaluation helper or protocol mirrors differ from the package after activation
fresh-task evidence lists more or less than one active codex-project-workflow entry
negative or standard context-load budgets regress
approval refers to a different target or scope
